description: time_origin_mode is exposed and reported but never consumed in fitting logic; t0 remains hardcoded to first sample and picker behavior.
impact: Reported configuration can differ from executed behavior.

description: fit_window_mode is exposed and reported but window behavior is fixed to pickRelaxWindow implementation plus fractional trims.
impact: Audit metadata overstates controllability of window definition.

description: baseline_mode/interpolation_mode/smoothing_mode/derivative_mode/no_relax_threshold_mode are declared but not wired into core fit path.
impact: Core physics choices are documented as configurable but mostly inert.

description: derivative_fallback_fraction is declared (0.2 default) but picker uses literal 0.2 and ignores config.
impact: Cannot tune fallback transient exclusion from canonical config.

description: Field threshold differs by entry point: picker default 1 Oe, main script uses 0.5 Oe, fitAll default 20 Oe.
impact: Window start can shift between workflows, affecting inferred relaxation rates.

description: main_relaxation calls Plots_relaxation before fitting; with alignByDrop=true this mutates Time_table used later in fits.
impact: Visualization setting changes analysis t0 in interactive path.

description: Log model uses log(max(t,minTimeForLog)) on absolute t within fit window without re-zeroing to window start.
impact: Estimated S is sensitive to time-origin choices unrelated to intrinsic dynamics.

description: Fallback no-relax branch writes tau=Inf, but canonical summary counts no-relax via isnan(allFits.tau).
impact: n_no_relax is systematically undercounted in canonical reports.

description: Output table mixes log parameter S with KWW parameters tau/n under one schema, with NaNs depending on chosen model.
impact: No single physically consistent relaxation observable is enforced.

description: If no valid low-field segment is found, start time is chosen by minimum dM/dt and window set to 20% of trace.
impact: Can include transients or artifacts with no explicit field-removal guarantee.
